summary.summary: Update of localization resource Trio/Sources/Localizations/Main/Localizable.xcstrings from Crowdin (2026-07-03). The patch (commit 94bb9c312786, merged in b0370ba66eff) makes many translation updates: numerous Norwegian (nb-NO) strings replace the term “blodsukker” with “glukose” (multiple occurrences) and adjust other wording; several new localization keys and localized entries are added (examples in the diff include keys for the symbols “– –” and “·”, "%@ Device Alerts", and relative time strings "%dh ago", "%dm ago", "%ds ago"); some localization entries change state from "new" to "translated" for various languages; at least one block present previously for "(Snoozed)" and several snooze-duration entries (e.g., "1 hour", "3 hours", "20 min") are removed in the shown diff; a small comment change (removed the word "units" from "%@" comment) is present. All changes are in the Localizable.xcstrings file according to the provided diff and file record.
summary.userFacingImpact: These are localization/string-table changes only (file: Trio/Sources/Localizations/Main/Localizable.xcstrings). Users will see updated text in the UI (notably Norwegian wording changing from “blodsukker” to “glukose”) and new localized labels/phrases (device-alert label, punctuation keys, and relative time strings). Because some altered strings appear in warnings and alarm-related text, a human review of the updated safety-critical phrasing is recommended to confirm no unintended change to meaning or clinical guidance.
